### Initialize AGENTS.md in TUI (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Starts a guided setup process for creating or updating the 'AGENTS.md' file, defining project rules.

```bash
/init
```

--------------------------------

### Example Request-context Call

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

An example of a GET request to retrieve a file system tree, specifying a path, directory, and workspace.

```HTTP
GET /api/fs/tree?path=.&directory=/repo/app&workspace=ws_123
```

--------------------------------

### opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tools.mdx

Example configuration to allow the 'lsp' tool.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "permission": {
    "lsp": "allow"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Wayland Clipboard Utility for Linux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/troubleshooting.mdx

Install `wl-clipboard` on Wayland-based Linux systems to enable copy/paste functionality in OpenCode.

```bash
apt install -y wl-clipboard
```

--------------------------------

### opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tools.mdx

Example configuration to allow the 'skill' tool.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "permission": {
    "skill": "allow"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode server in WSL

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/windows-wsl.mdx

Command to start the OpenCode server in WSL, allowing external connections.

```bash
opencode serve --hostname 0.0.0.0 --port 4096
```

--------------------------------

### Install X11 Clipboard Utilities for Linux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/troubleshooting.mdx

Install `xclip` or `xsel` on X11-based Linux systems to enable copy/paste functionality in OpenCode.

```bash
apt install -y xclip
```

```bash
apt install -y xsel
```

--------------------------------

### Initiate Custom Provider Connection in OpenCode

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Starts the `/connect` command flow, allowing selection of 'Other' to begin configuring a custom OpenAI-compatible provider.

```bash
$ /connect

┌  Add credential
│
◆  Select provider
│  ...
│  ● Other
└
```

--------------------------------

### Configure server settings in opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/web.mdx

Example JSON configuration for server settings within the `opencode.json` config file.

```json
{
  "server": {
    "port": 4096,
    "hostname": "0.0.0.0",
    "mdns": true,
    "cors": ["https://example.com"]
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example tui.json Configuration File

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Illustrates a comprehensive `tui.json` configuration file for customizing TUI behavior, including theme, keybinds, scrolling, mouse settings, and attention notifications.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/tui.json",
  "theme": "opencode",
  "leader_timeout": 2000,
  "keybinds": {
    "leader": "ctrl+x",
    "command_list": "ctrl+p"
  },
  "scroll_speed": 3,
  "scroll_acceleration": {
    "enabled": false
  },
  "diff_style": "auto",
  "mouse": true,
  "attention": {
    "enabled": true,
    "notifications": true,
    "sound": true,
    "volume": 0.4,
    "sound_pack": "opencode.default",
    "sounds": {
      "error": "./sounds/error.mp3"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Clipboard for Headless Linux Environments

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/troubleshooting.mdx

Install `xvfb` and set up a virtual display to enable copy/paste functionality in OpenCode when running in a headless Linux environment.

```bash
apt install -y xvfb
```

```bash
Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24 > /dev/null 2>&1 &
export DISPLAY=:99.0
```

--------------------------------

### Register a Built-In Grep Tool with Permissions

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/tools.md

This example shows how a built-in tool, like `grep`, is constructed using `Tool.make`, capturing trusted services like `FileSystem` and `PermissionV2`, and asserting permissions during execution.

```ts
const filesystem = yield * FileSystem.Service
const permission = yield * PermissionV2.Service
const tools = yield * Tools.Service

yield *
  tools.register({
    grep: Tool.make({
      description: "Search file contents",
      input: Input,
      output: Output,
      execute: (input, context) =>
        Effect.gen(function* () {
          const root = yield* filesystem.resolveRoot(input)

          yield* permission.assert({
            sessionID: context.sessionID,
            agent: context.agent,
            source: {
              type: "tool",
              messageID: context.assistantMessageID,
              callID: context.toolCallID,
            },
            action: "grep",
            resources: [input.pattern],
            save: ["*"],
            metadata: { root: root.resource },
          })

          return yield* filesystem.grep(input, root)
        }).pipe(/* translate expected typed errors to ToolFailure */),
    }),
  })
```

--------------------------------

### Set a server password

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/web.mdx

Command to start the web server with a password set via an environment variable for authentication.

```bash
OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD=secret opencode web
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode TUI for Specific Directory (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Launches the OpenCode TUI for a project located at the specified file path.

```bash
opencode /path/to/project
```

--------------------------------

### Access Windows files from WSL

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/windows-wsl.mdx

Example of navigating to a Windows project directory from WSL and running OpenCode.

```bash
cd /mnt/c/Users/YourName/Documents/project
opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Secure OpenCode server with password

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/windows-wsl.mdx

Command to start the OpenCode server in WSL with a password for security.

```bash
OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD=your-password opencode serve --hostname 0.0.0.0
```

--------------------------------

### Initial Location Load and Background Plugin Activation Flow

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/catalog-config-plugin-lifecycle.md

This flow describes the initial setup of a location, including building layers from authored config and activating plugins in the background, which then trigger a debounced full service reload.

```ts
LocationServiceMap.get(ref)
  → build location layer
    → Config.layer reads authored documents
      → merge authored documents
      → run currently active Config transforms
    → Policy.layer reads transformed Config
    → Catalog.layer reads transformed Config
      → materialize baseline provider/model catalog
  → PluginBoot baseline ready
    → Frontend.fetchCatalog()

PluginBoot background fiber
  → install/update plugin packages concurrently
  → activate completed plugins
    → Config.transform()
      → transform(updateConfig)
    → ReloadScheduler.request()
      → debounce short burst of completed activations
      → Reload.all()
        → Config.get()
          → run newly active Config transforms
        → Catalog.reload()
          → Catalog.Event.Updated
            → Frontend.refetchCatalog()
```

--------------------------------

### Start New Session in TUI (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Clears the current conversation and starts a new OpenCode session.

```bash
/new
```

--------------------------------

### Specify a custom port

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/web.mdx

Command to start the web server on a specific port instead of a random available one.

```bash
opencode web --port 4096
```

--------------------------------

### Session-pinned prompt call example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

An example of a session-pinned API call, showing the HTTP method, path, and how the server resolves the session context.

```HTTP
POST /api/session/ses_123/prompt

// server resolves
sessionID -> { directory, workspaceID? }
```

--------------------------------

### Provider Use Action and Resource Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/provider-policy.md

This text snippet illustrates the basic action and resource format for controlling provider usage within the policy system.

```text
action:   provider.use
resource: provider ID, such as openai or company-ai
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode TUI in Current Directory (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Initiates the OpenCode terminal user interface for the project located in the current working directory.

```bash
opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Enter SAP AI Core Service Key Interactively

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Provide your SAP AI Core service key JSON when prompted by the Opencode CLI during the connection setup.

```txt
┌ Service key
│
│
└ enter
```

--------------------------------

### Attach a terminal TUI to a running web server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/web.mdx

Commands to start the web server and then attach a terminal user interface (TUI) to it, allowing simultaneous use of both interfaces.

```bash
# Start the web server
opencode web --port 4096

# In another terminal, attach the TUI
opencode attach http://localhost:4096
```

--------------------------------

### Calculate Model Availability Based on Provider and Model Status

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/provider-model.md

This example demonstrates the logic for determining if a model is available, which requires both the provider and the model itself to be enabled.

```ts
const available = provider.enabled !== false && model.enabled
```

--------------------------------

### ApiEvent Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

An example of a 'message.part.delta' event using the ApiEvent envelope structure.

```json
{
  "id": "evt_01",
  "type": "message.part.delta",
  "time": 1760000000000,
  "context": {
    "directory": "/repo/app",
    "workspaceID": "ws_123"
  },
  "payload": {
    "sessionID": "ses_123",
    "messageID": "msg_456",
    "partID": "part_789",
    "field": "text",
    "delta": "hello"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Public Surface Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/storage/effect-sqlite-package.md

Example usage of the Drizzle Effect SQLite adapter, mirroring Drizzle's API patterns.

```typescript
const db = yield * EffectDrizzleSqlite.make({ relations }).pipe(Effect.provide(EffectDrizzleSqlite.DefaultServices))

yield * db.select().from(users)
yield *
  db.transaction(
    (tx) =>
      Effect.gen(function* () {
        yield* tx.insert(users).values({ name: "Ada" })
      }),
    { behavior: "immediate" }
  )
```

--------------------------------

### Use OpenCode from WSL

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/windows-wsl.mdx

Navigate to a project directory on Windows from WSL and run OpenCode.

```bash
cd /mnt/c/Users/YourName/project
opencode
```

--------------------------------

### .ignore

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tools.mdx

Example content for a .ignore file to include specific directories in ripgrep searches.

```text
!node_modules/
!dist/
!build/
```

--------------------------------

### Select OpenAI Authentication Method

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Choose between ChatGPT Plus/Pro authentication or manually entering an API key for OpenAI.

```txt
┌ Select auth method
│
│ ChatGPT Plus/Pro
│ Manually enter API Key
└
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Filesystem and Tool Runtime Settings

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

This snippet shows how to configure formatters, language servers, attachment processing, and tool output limits in the v2 schema, including disabling built-ins and defining custom commands.

```jsonc
{
  "formatter": {
    "prettier": { "disabled": true },
    "project": { "command": ["./scripts/format", "$FILE"], "extensions": [".foo"] }
  },
  "lsp": {
    "typescript": { "disabled": true },
    "project": { "command": ["project-language-server", "--stdio"], "extensions": [".foo"] }
  },
  "attachments": {
    "image": { "auto_resize": true, "max_width": 2000, "max_height": 2000 }
  },
  "tool_output": { "max_lines": 2000, "max_bytes": 51200 }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Launch OpenCode Desktop with Wayland Support

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/troubleshooting.mdx

Use this environment variable prefix when launching OpenCode Desktop on Linux to enable Wayland support, which may resolve blank window or compositor errors.

```bash
OC_ALLOW_WAYLAND=1
```

--------------------------------

### Initial Catalog Load and Plugin Activation Flow

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/catalog-config-plugin-lifecycle.md

Details the sequence for building the initial catalog layer, activating immediately available plugins, and background activation of slow plugins, leading to catalog rebuilds.

```ts
LocationServiceMap.get(ref)
  → build location layer
    → Catalog.layer creates empty catalog state
    → PluginBoot.layer activates immediately available plugins
      → ConfigProviderPlugin installs Catalog.transform()
      → ModelsDevPlugin installs Catalog.transform()
      → AuthPlugin installs Catalog.transform()
    → Catalog.layer applies active transforms during boot
    → apply policy
    → materialize baseline provider/model catalog
  → PluginBoot baseline ready
    → Frontend.fetchCatalog()

PluginBoot background fiber
  → install/update plugin packages concurrently
  → activate completed plugins
    → Catalog.transform()
      → transform(updateCatalog)
        → Catalog internally rebuilds
          → Catalog.Event.Updated
            → Frontend.refetchCatalog()
```

--------------------------------

### Session Context Epoch Initialization and Update Flow

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/session.md

This diagram illustrates the sequence of interactions between the client, runner, system context registry, epoch store, session history, and LLM during context epoch management.

```text
Client            Runner                         System Context Registry       Context Epoch Store       Session History         LLM
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   ├─ Admit prompt ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────▶                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ├─ Observe initial context ────────────▶                           │                       │                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ◀─ Complete baseline or unavailable ───┤                           │                       │                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ├─ Initialize missing epoch ───────────────────────────────────────▶                       │                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ├─ Promote eligible input ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────▶                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ├─ Reconcile at safe boundary ─────────▶                           │                       │                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ◀─ Unchanged or chronological update ──┤                           │                       │                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ├─ Advance snapshot atomically with update ────────────────────────▶                       │                 │
   │                 │                                      │                           │                       │                 │
   │                 ├─ Baseline + chronological history ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────▶
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Ambient Instruction Sources (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

This snippet defines an array of local paths, glob patterns, or remote URLs for ambient instructions. Instructions are automatically included as model context.

```jsonc
{
  "instructions": [
    "CONTRIBUTING.md",
    "docs/guidelines.md",
    ".cursor/rules/*.md",
    "https://example.com/shared-rules.md"
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Advanced Custom Provider Configuration with API Key, Headers, and Limits

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Demonstrates advanced `opencode.json` settings for a custom provider, including `apiKey` via environment variable, custom `headers`, and model `limit` options for context and output tokens.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "provider": {
    "myprovider": {
      "npm": "@ai-sdk/openai-compatible",
      "name": "My AI ProviderDisplay Name",
      "options": {
        "baseURL": "https://api.myprovider.com/v1",
        "apiKey": "{env:ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}",
        "headers": {
          "Authorization": "Bearer custom-token"
        }
      },
      "models": {
        "my-model-name": {
          "name": "My Model Display Name",
          "limit": {
            "context": 200000,
            "output": 65536
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Snowflake Cortex Browser OAuth Interactive Prompts

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

These prompts guide you through selecting the authentication method and entering your Snowflake account identifier during the browser OAuth flow.

```txt
┌ Select auth method
│
│ Login with Snowflake (External Browser)
│ Paste PAT or bearer token manually
└
```

```txt
┌ Snowflake Account Identifier
│
│
└ enter
```

### Git Commit Message for TUI Package Skeleton

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/tui-package.md

This commit message indicates the addition of the standalone package skeleton for the TUI, marking the initial setup phase.

```text
feat(tui): add standalone package skeleton
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Plugins in v2

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

This snippet demonstrates how to configure plugins in the v2 schema, including specifying package strings and objects with options for ordered loading.

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": [
    "opencode-helicone-session",
    {
      "package": "@my-org/audit-plugin",
      "options": {
        "endpoint": "https://audit.example.com"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Reference Configured File Alias in TUI Message (Text)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Compares the current setup with content from a file referenced via a configured alias, adding its content to the conversation.

```text
Compare our setup with @docs/README.md
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Skill Discovery Sources (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

Use this snippet to define an array of local paths or remote URLs where skills can be discovered. Skills are loaded or invoked intentionally, providing reusable prompt workflows.

```jsonc
{
  "skills": ["./team-skills", "~/shared-skills", "https://example.com/.well-known/skills/"]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure MCP Servers with Local and Remote Definitions

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

This snippet illustrates configuring MCP servers, including global timeout defaults and specific local and remote server definitions with commands, environment variables, URLs, headers, and OAuth settings.

```jsonc
{
  "mcp": {
    "timeout": { "startup": 30000, "request": 300000 },
    "servers": {
      "github": {
        "type": "local",
        "command": ["npx", "-y", "@github/github-mcp-server"],
        "environment": { "GITHUB_TOKEN": "{env:GITHUB_TOKEN}" },
        "disabled": false,
        "timeout": { "startup": 60000 },
      },
      "docs": {
        "type": "remote",
        "url": "https://docs.example.com/mcp",
        "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer {env:DOCS_TOKEN}" },
        "oauth": {
          "client_id": "{env:MCP_CLIENT_ID}",
          "client_secret": "{env:MCP_CLIENT_SECRET}",
          "scope": "read write",
          "callback_port": 19876,
          "redirect_uri": "http://127.0.0.1:19876/mcp/oauth/callback",
        },
        "disabled": false,
        "timeout": { "request": 600000 },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/session/:sessionID/history

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/session.md

Retrieves a paginated list of historical durable session events for a given session ID. This endpoint provides a finite view of events, suitable for request/response consumers.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/session/:sessionID/history

### Description
Retrieves a paginated list of historical durable session events for a given session ID. This endpoint provides a finite view of events, suitable for request/response consumers. It returns only events in the public durable Session schema.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/session/:sessionID/history

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sessionID** (string) - Required - The unique identifier for the session.

#### Query Parameters
- **after** (integer) - Optional - An exclusive aggregate sequence cursor. Omission starts before sequence zero.
- **limit** (integer) - Optional - The maximum number of events to return. Defaults to 50, accepts at most 100.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **data** (array) - An array of public durable Session events. Callers derive the next 'after' from the final event's durable sequence when 'hasMore' is true.
- **hasMore** (boolean) - Indicates if there are more events available to fetch.
```

--------------------------------

### opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tools.mdx

Configuration to allow the 'webfetch' tool.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "permission": {
    "webfetch": "allow"
  }
}
```

### List Available Themes in TUI (Bash)

Displays a list of available themes for customizing the OpenCode TUI appearance.

```bash
/themes
```

### Display Help Dialog in TUI (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Shows the help dialog within the OpenCode TUI, listing available commands and shortcuts.

```bash
/help
```

### List Available Models in TUI (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Displays a list of all available language models that can be used with OpenCode.

```bash
/models
```

### opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tools.mdx

Configuration to allow the 'question' tool.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "permission": {
    "question": "allow"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Input API Key for Custom Provider in OpenCode

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Captures the API key for the custom provider. This step stores the credential, but further `opencode.json` configuration is required.

```bash
$ /connect

┌  Add credential
│
▲  This only stores a credential for myprovider - you will need to configure it in opencode.json, check the docs for examples.
│
◇  Enter your API key
│  sk-...
└
```

--------------------------------

### Execute SDK Build and Git Diff Checks

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/tui-package.md

These commands build the SDK and then perform a 'git diff --check' to verify no unintended changes or conflicts after SDK API modifications, ensuring stability.

```shell
./packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts
git diff --check
```

--------------------------------

### Enable websearch when launching OpenCode

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tools.mdx

Command to enable the 'websearch' tool by setting the OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA environment variable.

```bash
OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA=1 opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Define an Agent with Permissions in JSON Configuration

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

This snippet shows how to define an agent, including its model, options, description, system prompt, mode, and specific permissions within the 'agents' configuration block.

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "reviewer": {
      "model": "openrouter/openai/gpt-5",
      "variant": "high",
      "options": {
        "headers": { "x-agent": "reviewer" },
        "body": {},
        "aisdk": { "provider": {}, "request": { "reasoningEffort": "high" } },
      },
      "description": "Review changes for correctness",
      "system": "Find regressions and missing tests.",
      "mode": "subagent",
      "color": "warning",
      "steps": 12,
      "disabled": false,
      "permissions": [{ "action": "edit", "resource": "*", "effect": "deny" }],
    },
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Provider Selection Policies with JSONC

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

This snippet demonstrates how to use `experimental.policies` to define provider selection rules, denying all providers by default and then explicitly allowing 'anthropic'.

```jsonc
{
  "experimental": {
    "policies": [
      {
        "effect": "deny",
        "action": "provider.use",
        "resource": "*"
      },
      {
        "effect": "allow",
        "action": "provider.use",
        "resource": "anthropic"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### User-Global Configuration for Provider Policy

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/provider-policy.md

This snippet demonstrates a user-global configuration denying the use of the 'openai' provider, which overrides project-level settings due to policy precedence.

```jsonc
{
  "experimental": {
    "policies": [{ "effect": "deny", "action": "provider.use", "resource": "openai" }],
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Migrating Legacy Allowlist to v2 Policy

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/provider-policy.md

This snippet demonstrates how to convert a legacy 'enabled_providers' configuration into the v2 policy format, using a default deny and specific allows.

```jsonc
{
  "enabled_providers": ["anthropic", "openai"],
}
```

```jsonc
{
  "experimental": {
    "policies": [
      { "effect": "deny", "action": "provider.use", "resource": "*" },
      { "effect": "allow", "action": "provider.use", "resource": "anthropic" },
      { "effect": "allow", "action": "provider.use", "resource": "openai" }
    ],
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Project Configuration for Provider Policy

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/provider-policy.md

This snippet shows a project-level configuration allowing the use of the 'openai' provider.

```jsonc
{
  "experimental": {
    "policies": [{ "effect": "allow", "action": "provider.use", "resource": "openai" }],
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Sharing and Identity Settings (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/config.md

This snippet shows how to configure session sharing, enterprise URL for legacy sharing, and a display username override in the configuration.

```json
{
  "share": "disabled",
  "enterprise": { "url": "https://share.example.com" },
  "username": "developer"
}
```

--------------------------------

### List OpenCode Authentication Credentials

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Use this command to check if provider credentials are correctly added to your OpenCode configuration.

```shell
opencode auth list
```

--------------------------------

### opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tools.mdx

Configuration to allow the 'todowrite' tool.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "permission": {
    "todowrite": "allow"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Plugin Activation Triggering Catalog Rebuild

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/catalog-config-plugin-lifecycle.md

Illustrates how activating a plugin triggers a catalog transform, applying the plugin's logic and rebuilding the catalog with a diff commit.

```ts
Plugin.activate("acme-models")
  → Catalog.transform()
    → transform(applyAcmeToCatalog)
    → Catalog internally rebuilds
      → commit diff
```

--------------------------------

### Make OpenCode accessible on the network

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/web.mdx

Command to bind the server to all network interfaces, making it accessible from other devices on the network.

```bash
opencode web --hostname 0.0.0.0
```

--------------------------------

### Define a V2 Tool using Tool.make

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/tools.md

This snippet shows the `Definition` type and the `Tool.make` constructor used to create locally executable tools, specifying their input, output, and execution logic.

```ts
type Definition<Input, Output>
type AnyTool = Definition<any, any>

const make: <
  Input extends Schema.Codec<any, any, never, never>,
  Output extends Schema.Codec<any, any, never, never>,
>(config: {
  readonly description: string
  readonly input: Input
  readonly output: Output
  readonly execute: (
    input: Schema.Type<Input>,
    context: Tool.Context,
  ) => Effect.Effect<Schema.Type<Output>, ToolFailure>
  readonly toModelOutput?: (input: {
    readonly input: Schema.Type<Input>
    readonly output: Output["Encoded"]
  }) => ReadonlyArray<Tool.Content>
}) => Definition<Input, Output>
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Custom Provider Configuration in opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Configures a custom OpenAI-compatible provider in `opencode.json`, specifying the AI SDK package, display name, base URL, and a model definition.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "provider": {
    "myprovider": {
      "npm": "@ai-sdk/openai-compatible",
      "name": "My AI ProviderDisplay Name",
      "options": {
        "baseURL": "https://api.myprovider.com/v1"
      },
      "models": {
        "my-model-name": {
          "name": "My Model Display Name"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### sessions.create({ id?, location, ... })

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/session.md

Creates a new session or retrieves an existing one. An optional `id` can be provided; if omitted, an internal ID is generated. Reusing an existing `id` returns the corresponding session.

```APIDOC
## sessions.create

### Description
Creates a new session or retrieves an existing one. If an ID is omitted, an internal Session ID is generated. If a supplied ID is absent, a new Session is created. Reusing an existing ID returns the existing Session identity.

### Method Call
`sessions.create({ id?, location, ... })`

### Parameters
- **id** (string) - Optional - An optional Session ID. If omitted, one is generated. If supplied and absent, a new Session is created. If reused, the existing Session identity is returned.
- **location** (string) - Required - The location for the session.
- **...** (any) - Optional - Additional session properties.
```
